title: Is Stripping Instrumentality Project lost
wikitext:
[]: Is 'Stripping Instrumentality Project' "lost"?             Quote Post by shawnsg » Sun Apr 25, 2021 1:13 am I gave the forum rules a once over before making this, so I'll dance lightly in regards to the game content, and I'm not asking for anyone publicly sharing links, I just wanna discuss the availability of such an interesting game. What I'm wondering is, "Stripping Instrumentality Project" lost software? I've done a bit of digging on Google for "Stripping Instrumentality Project" and "エヴァと愉快な仲間たち 脱衣補完計画", but I haven't actually been able to find any evidence this game exists anywhere on the internet. It's going for 10k yen on Amazon Japan, but I've seen it listed for a lot lower on some of the other sites I came across (~630Y iirc?) but I have no idea the validity of any of these sites. Plenty of scans exist of the artbook, but it just seems strange that that such a... unique licensed game isn't on archive.org or more accessible. Maybe I'm just stupid, and someone can confirm it exists out there. Otherwise, I dunno, let me know your thoughts on the game I suppose.

[]: Re: Is 'Stripping Instrumentality Project' "lost"?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Apr 25, 2021 4:58 pm Finding a full emulation of the game will ostensibly be hard with Japanese copyright laws, but the usual suspect sites probably have framedumps on them if it's just the contents that you're after

Article: Is Stripping Instrumentality Project lost
[]: Is 'Stripping Instrumentality Project' "lost"?             Quote Post by shawnsg » Sun Apr 25, 2021 1:13 am I gave the forum rules a once over before making this, so I'll dance lightly in regards to the game content, and I'm not asking for anyone publicly sharing links, I just wanna discuss the availability of such an interesting game. What I'm wondering is, "Stripping Instrumentality Project" lost software? I've done a bit of digging on Google for "Stripping Instrumentality Project" and "エヴァと愉快な仲間たち 脱衣補完計画", but I haven't actually been able to find any evidence this game exists anywhere on the internet. It's going for 10k yen on Amazon Japan, but I've seen it listed for a lot lower on some of the other sites I came across (~630Y iirc?) but I have no idea the validity of any of these sites. Plenty of scans exist of the artbook, but it just seems strange that that such a... unique licensed game isn't on archive.org or more accessible. Maybe I'm just stupid, and someone can confirm it exists out there. Otherwise, I dunno, let me know your thoughts on the game I suppose.

[]: Re: Is 'Stripping Instrumentality Project' "lost"?             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Apr 25, 2021 4:58 pm Finding a full emulation of the game will ostensibly be hard with Japanese copyright laws, but the usual suspect sites probably have framedumps on them if it's just the contents that you're after
